$schema_note: Workspace-wide configuration. Fork this repository and edit this file (and repos.json / purposes/) for your own organization, OR run `mrw init <dir>` to scaffold a relocatable per-workspace copy under <dir>/.mrw/ (workspace.json, repos.json, purposes/, broker-policy.json) — scripts, the pre-push hook and `mrw` all discover the nearest ancestor .mrw/ by walking up from cwd (or $MRW_CONFIG_DIR if set), falling back to this tool checkout's config/ when no .mrw/ is found. This file (<toolHome>/config/workspace.json) is the default/fallback the scripts read when no per-workspace config is active.
allowed_push_orgs_note: GitHub orgs/users that git push is allowed to target, enforced by .githooks/pre-push in every cloned repo and worktree. Empty list = no restriction (a warning is printed on push).
allowed_push_hosts_note: Hosts a push may target. The pre-push hook checks BOTH host and org, so a remote like https://evil.example/ALLOWED_ORG/repo cannot pass by matching only the org segment.
ticket_source_note: Adapter used by /open-task to fetch ticket content into docs/task.md. One of the scripts in scripts/lib/ticket-sources/ (without .sh): 'manual' | 'github-issues'. Additional adapters can be dropped into that directory (see examples/ticket-sources/).
ticket_id_pattern_note: Ticket IDs must match this pattern. The prefix is required and never auto-added.
state_root_note: Absolute path where repositories/ and tasks/ live. Empty ⇒ the workspace base: in legacy mode (no .mrw/ active) that's the tool checkout (byte-for-byte identical to before this field existed); in workspace mode (a per-workspace .mrw/ is active — see $schema_note) that's the directory holding .mrw/. Config itself (this file, repos.json, purposes/, broker-policy.json) always lives in config_dir — <toolHome>/config by default/fallback, or <workspace-base>/.mrw/ when relocated with `mrw init`. Everything else (scripts/, templates/, harness/, .githooks/, .claude/, docker/, .devcontainer/) always stays in the tool checkout.
